notes: Installed base declined from ~87,000 sites (1992) to ~42,000 sites (1996); declining at 20%+ per year; hardware parts increasingly unavailable
Introduced October 1994; priced $9,800-$21,000; ran System/36 SSP applications; withdrew from marketing by 2000; Advanced/36 Machine support removed in OS/400 V4R5
Entry level ~$8,000; OS/400 V3R6/V3R7 Year 2000 compliant; evolved to iSeries (2000), System i (2006), IBM Power Systems running IBM i (2008-present)
PC-LAN migration option; $10,000/yr to manage NT server + $1,000/yr per attached PC by industry rule-of-thumb
V3R6 and V3R7 versions were Year 2000 compliant; evolved to IBM i (2008); still active as IBM i on Power Systems
Operating system for System/36 and Advanced 36; SSP applications ran in System/36 Environment on AS/400; Year 2000 compliant SSP apps were rare
Moves System/36 data to Advanced 36 at 45MB per hour; migration service ~$50/hour; average migration time one day
